23yr; preschool; asst teacher; nanny; babysitter; college student

Aria has a mixed background—1 year preschool assistant teacher plus informal nanny/babysitting and ballet instruction—as a University of Utah student. She demonstrates a thoughtful, compassionate approach to conflict resolution and offers part-time availability with full summer flexibility. Utah's lenient experience standards support an APPROVE with medium confidence.

Hello. My name is Arya green. I am 23 years old. I am currently a student at the University of Utah, studying modern dance and psychology, I grew up in Washington and Hawaii but I relocated to Utah to finish school. I have been working with children since I was 12 in,
 Informal babysitting settings as a nanny for family friends. But also as a assistant teacher for a preschool for about a year, I also teach ballet. So I work with children in that capacity as well, and yeah.
I am located in Salt Lake City Utah. But I am going to move to Centerville in a few months, so just around that area.

I am looking for part-time work. As I will also be a part-time student so just to put that in my schedule and then I will likely be available Monday Wednesdays and Fridays or a full eight to ten hour shift because I have classes on the other days.  Eight days. So,

So in a city and a situation where a child has hit another child, I will first make sure that the child who was hit was not is not in any immediate physical danger and that their emotional needs are being met either by another teacher or by myself. And then I will take the child who hit the other child aside and we'll set it a boundary. I will really emphasize the rule of not hitting other students.
 And I will try to help understand the emotions behind that behavior, and give them a moment to calm themselves down. Maybe take away, if they use something to hit the child, I'll take that away as a consequence and I will sit with the child while they
 Try to calm themselves down or I will give them a little bit of space if they request it and we will move forward.
